key_insights.insight: Idle time is essential for creativity because it allows the mind to wander and generate ideas without pressure.
key_insights.quote: If I'm staring out the window into space as the rest of us might say then it just looks like I'm staring into space but I'm probably having an idea for a song or a lyric or working something over in my head

key_insights.insight: External pressures and consequences can diminish the quality of creative work.
key_insights.quote: As soon as you do those things,kind of the quality starts to sort of lessen.

key_insights.insight: The environment in which you start your career can heavily influence your creative output.
key_insights.quote: The environment actually changed the creative because you you realize that attention you learned very early that attention is is the thing

key_insights.insight: Comparing yourself to others and constantly chasing external validation can hold your happiness hostage.
key_insights.quote: It's holding your happiness hostage, right? Kind of, yes.

key_insights.insight: Negative voices and self-criticism are unlikely to ever disappear completely, but therapy can help manage and contextualize them.
key_insights.quote: Therapy's taught me...Very sort of crucially,it's taught me that they're not going to go away entirely.

key_insights.insight: It's more valuable to diminish the power your insecurities have over you, rather than completely ridding yourself of them.
key_insights.quote: We shouldn't hold ourselves to the standard of completely ridding ourselves of our trauma or insecurities it's really about diminishing the power they have over you.

key_insights.insight: Learning to wade through your issues, confronting them head-on, can be more effective than trying to jump over them or ignore them.
key_insights.quote: I need to get better at getting in the water getting a bit, wading through against the currents that are trying to send me downstream

key_insights.insight: It's helpful to remind ourselves that life is long, and that there will always be more chances for success.
key_insights.quote: But like, so easy to slip into that mindset of like, it's all done.You've had your time.But life is long.I'm trying to remind myself of that.

key_insights.insight: Embracing a vulnerable approach to creating and expressing art can make it more relatable.
key_insights.quote: Your new music is testament of everything you've saidtoday it's it's it's evidence that you have unlocked a new sort of...created art that feels much more relatable because it's much more well-rounded

actionable_advice.advice: Schedule regular windows of idle time where you can relax and let your mind wander.
actionable_advice.how_to: Set aside 15-30 minutes each day to simply stare out the window, meditate, or engage in a relaxing activity without specific goals or deadlines.

actionable_advice.advice: Confront issues and negative self-talk by getting in the water.
actionable_advice.how_to: Find a professional or friend to discuss your issues with instead of trying to jump over them and avoid them. Try to be vulnerable and share your feelings so you can better process.

actionable_advice.advice: Don't fear trying new things and putting yourself in vulnerable positions.
actionable_advice.how_to: Embrace the philosophy of 'leap and the net will appear' by recognizing that even if you don't succeed at first, you will learn something new and be able to make adjustments.

memorable_stories: Anecdote about performing in noisy pubs and adapting his music to capture the audience's attention.
Story of Maggie Rogers' concert and Sam Smith's comments on the traumatic aspects of sudden success.
Story of working with the Bee Gees and Barry Gibb sharing wisdom about the ups and downs of a long career.
Busking in Brighton and learning what captures the audience.
contrarian_claims: That trying to rid ourselves of our trauma is less valuable than diminishing the power trauma has over us.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - James Bay: Imposter Syndrome, Trauma & Controlling The Voice In Your Head
